claim: A photograph shows one petri dish that was coughed on by a person not wearing a mask and another petri dish coughed on by the same person wearing a mask.

result.claim: A photograph shows one petri dish that was coughed on by a person not wearing a mask and another petri dish coughed on by the same person wearing a mask.
result.reasoning:
The claim describes a visual demonstration of a well-established scientific principle: that wearing a mask reduces the spread of respiratory droplets. Multiple high-credibility sources support this principle. A systematic review (Paper ID: 37611625, Credibility Rating: 5.0/5) found that 39 out of 45 observational studies showed mask-wearing was associated with a reduction in SARS-CoV-2 transmission (prop_278105, prop_278174). This review also explicitly states that wearing masks reduced the transmission of SARS-CoV-2 infection (prop_278248) and that higher quality masks (respirators) were even more effective (prop_278249, prop_278141, prop_278212). Additionally, other papers, though with lower credibility ratings, corroborate that face masks retain potentially contagious droplets (prop_269633, Paper ID: 6e1efd12cf6f767f6c3753282b13790b3ae84de1) and protect from vapor drops carrying viruses (prop_269608, Paper ID: W2028524298). The photograph would visually represent this scientific consensus.

**Evidence Note:** 37611625, 6e1efd12cf6f767f6c3753282b13790b3ae84de1, W2028524298

evidence.text: Chen et al. investigated masks for reducing transmission.

evidence.text: Bag-mask ventilation can be as effective as ventilation through an endotracheal tube for short periods.
